org: Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea

candidates.stmt: Sarah was elected to the Council in 2016, and has lived in Abingdon Ward for over 27 years with her husband and three children. She has chaired the Family and Childrens Services Scrutiny Commiitee and was more recently the Lead Member for Adult Social Care and Public Health.  Sarah is currently a Governor of Guy’s and St Thomas’ NHS Foundation Trust.

Anne has lived in Kensington all her adult life and went to school in Chelsea . She has been a Councillor since 2016 and became the Lead Member for Communities in 2018. Before becoming Lead Member, Anne sat as a Trustee of the Westway Trust. At the same time, Anne was a member of the Planning Committee and on Family and Children’s Services scrutiny. Anne is married with two children.

James is committed to serving all Abingdon’s residents and local businesses.  He has particular interests in education, planning and the quality of our streets and public spaces.  He is a Governor of St Philip’s and St Barnabas primary school and within the council, is Chairman of the Planning committee. He lives in the ward with his wife, Munna, and they are keen supporters of their local residents’ association, ESSA.

I am standing in the local elections for Hans Town & Brompton, an area I have lived in, grown up in and gone to school in.

This is not just somewhere I am campaigning, it is somewhere I genuinely care about.

I understand the character of the area, the pressures residents face and the importance of maintaining the high standards people rightly expect from the Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea.

I want to help ensure our streets remain clean and well maintained, that residents feel safe, supported and listened to and that the Council delivers the level of service expected in one of London’s most iconic and historic communities.

I am not a career politician. Alongside my professional work, I am standing because I believe local representation should come from people who are genuinely connected to the area and invested in its future.

Whether residents have lived here for decades or have recently made Hans Town & Brompton their home, I want to be a visible, approachable and proactive local voice for the community.

OUR PLEDGES TO YOU
• Protect local policing and community safety by installing more CCTV cameras
• Re-open Albert Bridge as soon as possible to all traffic
•Deliver continued investment in the Cremorne and World's End Estates to makes homes warmer and safer
• Protect our twice-weekly bin collections
• Maintain residents' boroughwide parking permits
• Continue to seize badly parked e-bikes and fine operators

Monica has lived in North Kensington since 1996 when she came back from directing Oxfam's post-war programmes in Iraq and she and her Iraqi husband chose to live in Colville because of the diversity and comfortable mix of cultures, and the vibrant character of Portobello Market.

She has an MSc in Social Policy and Planning and for 20 years managed pro-democracy programmes in the Middle East and Eastern Europe, representing local community interests with local and national government leaders. She retired from overseas work in 2008 and qualified as a UK welfare rights adviser and worked for a local advice bureau with Arabic speaking clients until 2015.

Monica was elected Councillor for Colville Ward in 2014.  She has been an active resident in Colville for many years and is currently Chair of the Colville Ward Forum, Deputy Chair of the Markets Streets Action Group, and a member of the Borough Voluntary Organisations Advisory Group.  At the Council Monica sits on Cabinet and Corporate Services, Housing and Property, and Public Realm Scrutiny Committees.

I've lived in London for 16 years, working in Housing with both Crisis and Hammersmith Council. As a committed human rights advocate, I've fought for voices that are often ignored. I know the housing system well, so I know how to drive change within it.

I grew up with damp and mould, waiting for urgent repairs that never came. My mum’s health suffered because of it. Now I work designing policy to tackle health inequalities, so that health systems work for everyone. That focus and approach is what I want to bring to Colville.

I’ve lived in Dalgarno for 27 years. I’ve worked with schools, community partners and youth services to tackle antisocial behaviour, keep young people in schools and demand support for children with SEND. I’m committed to defending our most vulnerable residents and fighting for a safer, fairer and more resilient community.

I was born here, so I’m clear eyed about the issues we face and the work needed to address them. Professionally, I’ve worked with councils, frontline workers, and schools, to ensure kids from disadvantaged backgrounds can thrive. I know what can be achieved when someone listens and then takes action.

As a Dalgarno resident for the past 11 years I have a deep understanding of your local issues, I have always been passionate about helping others, and I hope that you can give me the opportunity to serve you as your local representative. I am committed to working hard to make Dalgarno a better place for everyone, and I will always listen to your concerns and ideas. Together, we can create a brighter future for our community. As a computer science graduate, I hope to bring my technical skills and knowledge to the table to help solve some of the challenges that our community faces, as well as providing a perspective from the younger generation that is severely under-represented in local politics. I am dedicated to promoting inclusivity, diversity, and equality in our community, and I will work tirelessly to ensure that everyone's voice is heard. I believe that by working together, we can create a stronger, more vibrant community that we can all be proud of.

I was raised in Kensington, and this community has shaped who I am. I’ve seen first hand the challenges families face, and I’ve spent years working locally to support residents and strengthen our neighbourhood. I chaired our local residents’ association for two and a half years, led our Area Review Board, worked in RBKC’s voluntary sector, and carried out casework for our former MP, Emma Dent Coad. These experiences have grounded my commitment to listening, acting, and delivering for our community.

I founded Homemade Teachers, an organisation that supports parents in helping their children succeed and better understand the primary curriculum. This is because when families are empowered, our whole community thrives. As a teacher, I’ve taken GCSE students abroad to broaden their horizons and explore different cultures. I also volunteered internationally during my gap year in North India and organised annual excursions to Spain, experiences that deepened my commitment to equality, opportunity, and global responsibility.

I am a graduate of Queen Mary University of London, with a postgraduate degree from the University of Cambridge, specialising in Geography and Education. I bring a strong commitment to social and environmental justice.

It would be a privilege to represent the residents of Dalgarno Ward. I am determined to ensure that the voices of those living in the north of the borough are heard, and that every concern is met with fairness and persistence.

At the moment we are being let down.
 The council’s recent low grading from the social housing regulator highlights serious failures to meet Decent Homes standards. This is unacceptable, especially in a ward where more than 65% of residents live in social housing. Everyone deserves a safe, warm, and healthy home. Without it, our health, our children’s education, and our opportunities all suffer.

Together, we can achieve.
 Let’s build a greener, fairer, and more compassionate future for Dalgarno.

I worked as a business manager until my youngest daughter was diagnosed with a serious illness. I then became her Carer for 22 years. During those years, I decided to create something positive for us to do, and started building and running community projects, working with the Afro-Caribbean community in events and business start-ups, young adults in employment training, and children in performance, arts, crafts and cookery.

I was then invited to work with the local Council, Police and Probation, helping to create music and employement experience projects to help young people into work or college, and into useful, fulfilling lives. I took over the community safety strategy for a local estate and localised crime fell by 25% in the first year. We did this by partnership, community-building projects and tons of fun! I definitely hope to do more of this in Dalgarno!

In my spare time, my husband, who had come to England as a refugee,  and I ran a refugee drop-in centre, providing translation, support, and assistance to new refugees. We accompanied them to interviews and helped them settle into a new country more easily with group support and a welcome into our home for a hot meal of something from their own country.

In recent years, having seen how inaccessible the local areas can be for disabled residents, I have worked with Councils, developers and planners to change their vision of what true 'accessibility' is. Moving beyond access, I would like to see everyone in our community being able not just to get around but to have a real say in what we build locally. Let's go beyond mere access to agency.

I am now involved in the development of small housing properties in a voluntary position, and in community safety. As a Neighbourhood Watch Coordinator for 14 years, I take a keen interest in networking and community building to not only help one another but to ensure our areas stay safe and people feel cared for.

I am standing as a Candidate for Reform UK in Dalgarno Wa

I have lived in Golborne for over 30 years, and was a councillor between 2018 - 2022. I know what it’s like to be forced to fight for access to a safe home. I also serve on my local Resident Steering Group and as a North Kensington Law Centre trustee.

As your councillor since 2018, I’ve worked with residents on numerous campaigns, from saving Morley College, to protecting Edenham & Trellick. I studied biology at ImperiaI, and I believe that safe and healthy housing is the bedrock of public health.

I’ve lived locally for two decades, working in Compliance to make sure standards are met and people are safe. I’m on the board of my block of flats and I know how complex housing can be. Having worked for an MP and studied law, I know how to achieve change.

I am Mona Adam, the Green Party parliamentary candidate for Kensington and Bayswater constituency, I have lived in several countries and multi-cultural communities.

I was educated in Saudi Arabia where I studied sociology at King Saudi University. I later worked for the British Council, where I was responsible for establishing and running a Women's Centre.

Since moving to the UK, I have worked with several NGOs liaising with different agencies to implement policies and establish a rehabilitation centre that combats human trafficking.

I am standing for:

1.    Cost of living crisis:  This includes fuel and food poverty. Pensioners, people with disabilities and families on benefit come off the worst. Women are the worst affected in all categories.

2.    Housing: Implementing rent control measures and addressing housing inequality to ensure that everyone in our community has a place to call home. Building more council homes, fair rents, landlord register, homes built to decent homes standard including proper insulation and heating to reduce carbon emissions. This for all rental, council, and social housing properties and a new Rent Act to remove no fault evictions and section 21.

3.    NHS: Better health services from GP to hospital, money for upgrading and rebuilding our hospitals, more NHS dentists, better mental health services with the Gordon Hospital beds saved, palliative care with the Pembridge beds saved, better and more social care services.

4.    Immigration: I understand the importance of compassionate and fair immigration policies. I will work towards creating an inclusive society that welcomes diversity, protects the rights of immigrants, and fosters a sense of belonging.

5.    Homelessness: Tackling homelessness is a priority. I will work towards implementing comprehensive solutions, including affordable housing programs, support services for mental health and addiction, and collaborations with local organizations to address the root causes of home

Ellie has lived in London for over twenty years. She has worked in fashion all her adult life, working as a stylist and designing her eponymous dress label. She has always loved Portobello Road for its vibrancy and its individuality, often frequenting it through her work and with friends.

A strong believer in supporting small businesses, Ellie loves the brilliant offering of independent stores, restaurants and businesses of the Portobello area. Having always worked for herself she understands the struggles many have faced during this pandemic, and is keen to ensure local businesses are getting all the support they need.

Ellie volunteers with Dad’s House food bank, helping to deliver to local families across North Kensington, and with Solidarity Sports, working with local children. She has seen how mindfulness can have such a positive effect on children. Mental Health awareness is incredibly important to her and she encourages people to enjoy a more healthy lifestyle, walking and exercising in the wonderful green spaces we have all around us.
Krzysztof Ziembla

If re-elected, we will:
1. save the trees on Holland Park Avenue from being felled by the Mayor of London
2. ban coaches and heavy goods vehicles from Holland Park and Holland Park Mews
3. keep acoustic cameras on Holland Road to fine drivers of noisy vehicles
4. support Russell Road residents with noise and nuisance from Olympia, in LBHF
5. support Lorne Gardens residents affected by flooding
6. install CCTV on Addison Road and Napier Road, and keep it on Hansard Mews

And in K&C, we will:
1. protect twice-weekly bin collections
2. protect the boroughwide residents' parking permit
3. invest in our amazing spaces and parks, such as Holland Park
4. invest in clean streets - more jet washing, graffiti busting
5. deliver high quality social care for adults and children (both rated Outstanding)
6. fight for the best deal for residents, against Gov cuts and police stn closures

I’m a long-standing Kensington resident, working in healthcare research, with extensive business management experience. Having volunteered in education and mental health, I am passionate about giving back. I will ensure local concerns are listened to and addressed with practical and effective solutions.

I’m a lifelong Kensington resident, I volunteer at the Nourish Hub on St Ann’s Road. I’m standing in Holland Ward to hold the council accountable, fight for real change, and be a councillor who shows up and listens.

I’m a local business owner and strategy consultant, with leadership experience in the energy and charity sectors. I’m pragmatic, results-driven and committed to fixing broken services, ensuring value for taxpayers, and delivering safer, cleaner neighbourhoods.

Stephanie is standing as the Conservative candidate in the Norland Ward by-election, being held on 2nd May 2024. Stéphanie has resided in London for over 25 years, having lived in Kensington with her husband and children since 2008. Her community work has included supporting local food banks, fundraising for school scholarships, and serving as a Kensington resident liaison with the Independent Advisory Group at the MET. Stéphanie has played a key role in advocating for police presence across the Borough, and vitally, a continued police presence at Notting Hill Police Station. She has also been actively campaigning to keep Holland Park tube station operational and to oppose the current form of the Holland Park Roundabout project.

Current councillor and leader of the Labour Group, I have lived here for over two decades. I work in education, chair the Al-Manaar mosque,  and make delicious Kensington Hot Sauce. I want my neighbours to be treated with respect and dignity.

A resident here for over 30 years, former researcher and lecturer, long-standing community campaigner, and witness to the Grenfell Tower Fire. As your councillor, I've been pursuing justice, accountability and change for residents, our lives and our wellbeing.

I grew up here. I’m a councillor, Eco Church project Chair, and food bank trustee. I founded the African Caribbean Cultural Centre & work a lot with Bay20. I’ve been standing up on housing disrepair, community safety, and support for all those affected by Grenfell.

Like many people, I’ve reached my limit! The pressures we’re facing – such as cost of living, housing shortages, and inequality – keep growing, while the things we rely on – like local services, safe and adequate housing, clean air – aren’t getting the attention they need.

We all deserve better, and this is especially true for the people in Notting Dale. The Notting Dale community has incredible diversity, culture, and resilience – but it also faces real challenges, from the injustice of Grenfell to high climate risk.

It’s time that Notting Dale sees positive change. But change doesn’t work when it’s done TO a community. Instead, it has to be WITH them – and this will shape my approach as your Green councillor.

I will stand up for your concerns and make sure they’re dealt with. I will listen, be visible and accessible, and give residents the chance to SHAPE decisions, not just respond to them. I’ll fight for better housing, improved local services, accountability from the council, and a better quality of life for everyone.

As for me personally, I’m a lifelong West Londoner with Arab heritage. I’m a fierce campaigner for LGBTQIA+ rights, environmental action, social and economic justice, and an end to the suffering in Palestine, Congo, and Sudan. That’s why I’ve chosen to stand with the Green Party – because they are THE party standing up for fairness, equality, and a kinder, better future for everyone.

1) I will OPPOSE the re-introduction of ETRO Traffic Scheme if there is no public consultation or community consent.

2) I will campaign to INCREASE ENFORCEMENT of speed restrictions on the Netherhampton Road, so it is safe for all children on their way to school. 

3) I will help to ensure Wiltshire Council is CARBON NEUTRAL by 2030.

4) I will campaign for a CRACK DOWN on local crime, drug abuse, and anti-social behaviour.

5) I will help IMPROVE ACCOUNTABILITY in how our hard earned money is spent in Harnham.

6) I OPPOSE over-development around already built up residential areas, and want to see us meet our housing requirements in a new settlement which is a part of the Salisbury community, but separate from the Salisbury centre.

I have been living in Notting Hill for the last 8 years, right here in Pembridge for the last 4. I love our community here, the most vibrant and caring in London!

Previously I worked at a local charity, helping the most vulnerable residents in our area. I have been on the committee of the Pembridge Association - our residents association - for the last 4 years, helping to keep our conservation area running well for all its residents. I now teach at a community youth theatre in North Kensington, as well as my work as a freelance writer.

I am incredibly excited to be standing for the Liberal Democrats in Queen’s Gate. As your Liberal Democrat councillor, I will fight for you and our community building trust, safeguarding our history and empowering our citizens to play active and meaningful roles across Queen’s Gate. After many decades of Conservative rule, we believe that the time is now for a change and to vote for a sensible party that will not rest on their laurels or take the electorate’s support for granted. I believe the Liberal Democrats are that change and that’s why I am standing on May 7th.

Pawel Urbanski is the Liberal Democrat candidate for Queen’s Gate. He is a business owner and local employer with practical experience of building organisations, solving problems and working with people from different backgrounds. He has been closely involved in the local community and values the role that small businesses play in making the borough stronger, more vibrant and more resilient. He wants a borough that works first for residents, a council that takes safer neighbourhoods seriously, and a cleaner, better-run borough for everyone who lives and works here.

My name is Tom Bennett, and I am your Conservative candidate for Ealing Southall. 

My priorities for Ealing Southall are reducing crime, opposing new tower blocks, improving air quality, and supporting local businesses.

Crime in Ealing is up by 10% year on year, but the Conservatives are fighting back, through hiring 20,000 new police officers nationwide, and giving them greater powers of arrest and stop and search, but also through establishing a Prison Education Service that will give ex-convicts the skills they need to enter the workforce once they leave prison.

Tower blocks aren’t the answer to the lack of housing.  As your MP, I will push for good quality low-rise developments in our constituency, not towers like those proposed near West Ealing Station, which are marketed abroad, unsuitable for local families, and completely out of keeping with the area.

I would push the local Council to take advantage of the clean-air subsidies that are being proposed by the Conservative Government, and plant hundreds of new trees, and install 2,000 new electric-car charging points across the borough. 

I am a small business owner myself, and I will always stand up for our local business. Once elected, I would be proud to vote for the Conservative policy to cut business rates for small businesses, a move which would significantly reduce the burden on the large majority of our businesses in Ealing Southall.

I first came to Southampton 33 years ago and over the years I have seen how fantastic Southampton is to live and work in. With excellent universities, a world class port and a historic football team, Southampton is a very special city. I have recently moved to Southampton. 

In my twenties I served for six years in the British Army Reserves with the Honourable Artillery Company and got to understand what hard work, dedication, and public service are all about. If elected I will be your local champion and voice in Westminster. My objective is to ensure that Southampton, as the largest city in Hampshire, receives the resources it needs to continue to be a first class city and a destination for business investment, students and families.

My parents were both born in Ghana and they moved to London in the 1960s. I was born and raised in London. My parents instilled in me conservative values of helping others and hard work.

After reading physics at Durham University I started working in London. I later spent many years living and working in Hong Kong. I have always been passionate about giving back and helping others. More than a decade ago, I founded a charity to distribute solar lights to people without electricity in Africa, so that they can study or work after dark. Something we take for granted. Helping young people with their education is a cause that I am passionate about. I also volunteer at an almshouse and support army veterans who live alone.

As a local councillor I work every day for my 6,000 constituents dealing with local issues that matter to them. For example, stopping crime in stairwells of flats, improving housing by installing double-glazed windows, and providing support to those that need help with the cost of living.

Local residents have told me that they want to keep the street lights on at night and fix the pot holes. I will support Donna Jones, Police and Crime Commissioner, to make sure that Hampshire Police have the resources they need to ser

I am standing in the local election as I have lived here since 2005. During this time the Conservatives have been in power and I believe they have become too comfortable . They have run up a debt of £520 million , evidence that they are “ living beyond our means” . 
I believe that this council can be run more efficiently , and there is ample scope to make savings. With over 2,500 staff we can start with that. However certain policies I would not want to change and would fight hard to keep , twice weekly bin collections and borough wide parking but the 20 mph apart from selected areas , hospitals , schools and black spots I would ban. So my moto is “ Kind yes but always be fair and responsible”

I am a campaigner and community organiser living and working in North Ken. I have seen first-hand the issues that matter to people: poor-quality housing, graffiti and fly-tipping, and out of control e-bikes. I will be an accessible, hardworking councillor, who fights on behalf of residents and secures real change for St Helen’s.

I come from a family of veterans so I know what it means to serve. I work on energy investment projects and previously worked abroad, designing economic development programmes. I understand how to bring diverse communities and organisations together to improve lives. I will use  this experience to advocate you and your neighbours in St Helen’s.

I was born in Malawi and have lived in London most of my life with periods in Europe.

I have come out of retirement because I have 6 children and 4 grandchildren. I want them to enjoy a safe world and not the one that is otherwise coming.

I have something to offer as a politician with a vast wealth of business experience and everyday application of current affairs on a domestic and international basis at my fingertips.

I have noticed a complete difference of character in this country. It's changed. London has changed. I am here to support the Reform party and Farage to listen to the views of my constituents and be the voice and platform for Kensington and Bayswater.
